_meta.source: NCSL State Medical Cannabis Laws (ncsl.org/health/state-medical-cannabis-laws, updated 2026-06-01); DISA Marijuana Legality by State (disa.com/marijuana-legality-by-state, updated 2026-07-01)
_meta.notes: 0 = illegal (no comprehensive medical or adult-use program; CBD/low-THC-only laws do not count), 1 = medical use legal (comprehensive medical program for qualifying patients), 2 = recreational use legal for adults 21+. State law changes frequently; verify before relying on for decisions. Flagged as recently changed or not yet fully operational as of 2026-07: AL, GA, TX upgraded to comprehensive medical status within the prior ~12 months; NE approved medical cannabis by ballot measure (2024) but licensing/rulemaking was still incomplete with no dispensaries open as of mid-2026; VA has legal personal possession/home cultivation but no commercial retail market until a planned 2027-07-01 launch.
